{"key_event":"Civic mobilization amid M23 resurgence","reasoning":"Civil society became somewhat more capable through youth movements, Catholic networks, NGOs, digital activism, and election monitoring, but repression, internet shutdowns, disputed elections, and armed-group coercion kept it in the weak range. State power remained weak: presidential centralization, mining revenues, and formal continuity improved executive control, but underfunded decentralization, contested legitimacy, and the M23/ADF/CODECO security crisis reduced effective territorial authority.","society_change":0.4,"society_power":3.8,"state_change":-0.2,"state_power":3.6}
